passage:
I was raised by a teacher. My mother is a professor of early education. And from the time I went to kindergarten through my senior year in high school, I went to public schools. I wouldn't trade that education and experience for anything.

I had incredible teachers. As I look at my life today, the things I value most about myself- my imagination, my love of acting, my passion for writing, my love of learning, my curiosity- all come from how I was parented and taught.

And none of these qualities that I've just mentioned, none of these qualities that I prize so deeply, that have brought me so much joy, that have brought me so much professional success- none of these qualities that make me who I am can be tested.

I said before that I had incredible teachers. And that's true. But it's more than that. My teachers were EMPOWERED to teach me. Their time wasn't taken up with a bunch of test prep-this silly drill and kill nonsense that any serious person knows doesn't promote real learning.

No, my teachers were free to approach me and every kid in that classroom like an individual puzzle. They took so much care in figuring out who we were and how best to make the lessons resonate with each of us. They were empowered to unlock our potential. They were allowed to be teachers.
question: The writer salutes his teachers for all this EXCEPT:
explanation: The passage mentions that the teachers helped develop his imagination, curiosity, and creative skills (love of acting and writing). It does not mention them disciplining his temperament.

question: The literary device used in the line- approached every kid in class ‘like an individual puzzle' is:
explanation: A simile is a figure of speech that compares two different things using the words 'like' or 'as'. Here, comparing a kid to a puzzle using 'like' is a simile.

question: The extract is a/an ____ of teachers.
explanation: A eulogy is a speech or piece of writing that highly praises someone. This entire passage serves as a tribute and high praise for the writer's teachers.

question: The writer's teachers were empowered because they :

passage:
The recently launched Mission ShakthiSAT, is an ambitious initiative that aims to empower young girls worldwide to excel in space science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). This historic all-girls mission, led by Space Kidz India (SKI), involves a spacecraft journey from lunar-to-lunar orbit and will be part of ISRO's Chandrayaan-4 mission in 2026. Through this mission, SKI seeks to inspire young women to lead in space sciences and demonstrate their potential in STEM fields.

Space Kidz India has a proven track record, having launched 19 Near Space Launch Vehicles, 3 Suborbital Payloads, and 5 Orbital Satellites. The mission's goal is to provide girls with the opportunity to contribute to an actual space mission and witness their satellite journey to the Moon. Srimathy Kesan, Founder and CEO of SKI, emphasized that Mission ShakthiSAT is about creating a global platform where young women can innovate and lead in space sciences. The mission represents the limitless potential of women and their role in shaping the future of space exploration.

Meridian Space Command, a UK-based company, has partnered with SKI for mission management and in-space transportation. Using its orbital transfer vehicle, Meridian will efficiently transport the orbiter and lander to lunar orbit via PSLV, with a planned crash landing on the Moon.

The ShaktiSAT programme also includes an online training curriculum on space technology fundamentals, spacecraft systems, and payload development. Following the online courses, 108 girls from 108 countries will be selected to participate in intensive hands-on training in India, where they will collaborate to design, build, and test a satellite for the mission.
question: How will the ShaktiSAT mission facilitate global collaboration in space?
options: By involving girl participants from 108 countries, for hands-on training in India.
By building the first international space station in collaboration with other space agencies.
By launching individual satellites from all the 108 countries involved.
By involving 108 countries in funding the ambitious and one of its kind mission.
explanation: The passage explicitly states that '108 girls from 108 countries will be selected to participate in intensive hands-on training in India, where they will collaborate to design, build, and test a satellite for the mission.'

question: What is the primary aim of Mission ShakthiSAT?
options: To launch a satellite to planet Mars
Empowering young girls to lead and excel in space science
To develop a new type of spacecraft for deep space missions
To create a new International Space Station
explanation: The very first sentence of the passage states that the initiative 'aims to empower young girls worldwide to excel in space science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM).'

question: What is the significance of the Chandrayaan-4 mission in relation to ShakthiSAT?
options: It is absolutely unrelated to Mission ShakthiSAT's objectives.
It will carry the first Indian woman astronaut to the Moon.
It is going to be the first mission to use the ShaktiSAT spacecraft.
It's student-built satellite will be launched as part of this mission in 2026
explanation: The text explains that the student-built historic all-girls spacecraft journey 'will be part of ISRO's Chandrayaan-4 mission in 2026.'

question: What educational component does the ShaktiSAT programme provide for young girls before their hands-on training?
options: An opportunity to research and study asteroid mining.
An online training curriculum on space technology fundamentals.
A practical course on space shuttle piloting.
explanation: The passage clearly states, 'The ShaktiSAT programme also includes an online training curriculum on space technology fundamentals, spacecraft systems, and payload development' which happens before the hands-on training.

passage:
One afternoon in late June, as the sun shone warmly into the large courtyard, a very elegant Victoria, drawn by two sleek black horses, stopped in front of the mansion. The Countess descended the steps just as her husband, returning home, appeared at the carriage entrance.

The Count paused, his face turning slightly pale as he observed his wife. She was strikingly beautiful, with her long oval face, an ivory-like complexion, large gray eyes, and jet-black hair. Her graceful and aristocratic demeanour only heightened her appeal. Without sparing him a glance or acknowledging his presence, she entered the carriage with a high-bred air that rekindled the burning jealousy which had long consumed him.

Approaching her, he asked in a measured tone, "You are going for a drive?"

She responded coldly, "You see I am."

"May I join you?" he ventured, attempting to mask his unease.

"The carriage belongs to you," she replied disdainfully, her tone cutting and dismissive.

Without reacting to her hostility, he climbed in and gave the order: "To the Boulogne Woods" The footman took his place beside the coachman, and the spirited horses pranced onto the street. Husband and wife sat stiffly side by side in silence, the tension between them palpable. He hesitated, seeking a way to begin a conversation, but her resolute, icy demeanour held him back.

Finally, as if by accident, his gloved hand brushed hers. She recoiled sharply, her gesture laden with unmistakable disgust. He remained thoughtful, in spite of his usual authoritative and despotic character, and finally said: "Gabrielle!"
question: What does the Count's behaviour throughout the scene suggest about his personality?
options: He is indifferent and dismissive towards his wife
He is authoritative and controlling but also vulnerable to jealousy
He is confident and not bothered about the Countess’s demeanour
He is carefree and uninterested in his wife's opinions or interests
explanation: The text describes the Count's 'usual authoritative and despotic character' while also noting that his wife's actions 'rekindled the burning jealousy which had long consumed him,' showing he is controlling but vulnerable to jealousy.

question: Which of the following best describes the atmosphere inside the carriage?
options: Calm and amicable, with exchange of polite conversation
Tense and silent, filled with unresolved emotions
Cheerful, light and lively, with both sharing an easy camaraderie
Toxic but hopeful, with efforts at reconciliation
explanation: The passage states that they 'sat stiffly side by side in silence, the tension between them palpable' and her demeanour was icy, indicating a tense and silent atmosphere filled with unresolved emotions.

question: What characteristic of the Countess reignited the count's jealousy?
options: her dismissive response to his question
her choice of the Bois de Boulogne as the destination
her sharp tone when speaking to the coachman.
explanation: The passage clearly states, 'she entered the carriage with a high-bred air that rekindled the burning jealousy'. A 'high-bred air' refers directly to her elegance and aristocratic demeanour.

question:
Match the words used in the passage in List-I with their meanings in List-II

List–I (Words)
(A) appeal
(B) consumed
(C) palpable
(D) rekindled
(E) disdainful

List–II (Meanings)
(I) contemptuous, dismissive
(II) filled one with strong feelings, overwhelmed
(III) revived and awakened again
(IV) a quality that makes somebody or something interesting
(V) easily noticed by the mind or senses

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
explanation: Appeal means an interesting quality (A-IV). Consumed means overwhelmed by strong feelings (B-II). Palpable means easily noticed or felt (C-V). Rekindled means revived (D-III). Disdainful means contemptuous (E-I).

question:
Select the correct antonym of the underlined word from the options given below:

Radha is known for her lackasdaisical attitude but her ‘avid’ interest in the project has surprised one and all.
explanation: 'Avid' means having or showing a keen interest in or enthusiasm for something. Its antonym is 'averse', which means having a strong dislike or opposition to something.

question: Select the most appropriate antonym for the word “lament."
explanation: 'Lament' means to express passionate grief or sorrow. Its antonym is 'rejoice', which means to feel or show great joy.

question:
Rearrange the given parts in the correct sequence to form a meaningful sentence.

(A) grammar grows and changes
(B) correct use of English for the past, present and future
(C) and there is no such thing as
(D) In other words
explanation: The logical sequence is: 'In other words (D), grammar grows and changes (A), and there is no such thing as (C) correct use of English for the past, present and future (B).'

question:
Choose the best option to fill in the blank with the correct phrasal verb.

The manager asked the team to ___ the research and present their findings by next week.
explanation: 'Carry out' is a phrasal verb meaning to perform or complete a task, such as conducting research.

question: Choose the word similar in meaning to the word-Proclivity
explanation: 'Proclivity' refers to a tendency to choose or do something regularly, an inclination or predisposition toward a particular thing.

question:
Rearrange the given parts in the correct sequence to form a meaningful sentence.

(A) and enabling new innovations that
(B) various industries, enhancing efficiency
(C) were once thought impossible
(D) artificial intelligence is revolutionizing
explanation: The correct logical sequence is: 'Artificial intelligence is revolutionizing (D) various industries, enhancing efficiency (B) and enabling new innovations that (A) were once thought impossible (C).'

question:
Match the words in List-I with their definitions in List-II

List-I (Words)
(A) Convalescence
(B) Euphoria
(C) Verisimilitude
(D) Oblivion

List-II (Meanings)
(I) A state of having passed out of the memory, or being forgotten
(II) A period of time when you get well again after an illness or medical operation
(III) A strong feeling of happiness and excitement that usually lasts only a short time
(IV) Appearing true or real
explanation: Convalescence is recovery from illness (A-II). Euphoria is intense happiness (B-III). Verisimilitude is the appearance of being true (C-IV). Oblivion is the state of being forgotten (D-I).

question:
Match the figures of speech in List-I with their meanings in List-II

List-I (Figures of Speech)
(A) Alliteration
(B) Onomatopoeia
(C) Oxymoron
(D) Irony

List-II (Meanings)
(I) A phrase that combines contradictory words
(II) A contrast between what is said and what is meant
(III) Repetition of initial consonant sound in close succession
(IV) Words that imitate natural sounds
explanation: Alliteration is consonant repetition (A-III). Onomatopoeia consists of sound-imitating words (B-IV). An Oxymoron combines contradictions (C-I). Irony is saying one thing but meaning the opposite (D-II).

question:
Choose the correct option to fill in the blank.

Some of the people ____ to the party can't come.
explanation: The sentence requires a relative clause in the passive voice to properly modify 'the people'. 'Who were invited' acts as the correct relative clause.

question:
Match the idioms given in List-I with their meanings given in List-II

List-I (Idiom)
(A) Off the top of one's head
(B) Take issue with
(C) Pay lip service to
(D) Keep one's head

List-II (Meaning)
(I) to pretend to agree with
(II) to remain calm and sensible
(III) without much thought about correctness
(IV) to disagree with
explanation: 'Off the top of one's head' means stating something without careful thought (A-III). 'Take issue with' means to disagree (B-IV). 'Pay lip service' means insincere agreement (C-I). 'Keep one's head' means to stay calm (D-II).

question:
Choose the best option to fill in the blank with the correct phrasal verb.

She was upset when she found out that her manuscript had been ____ by the publisher.
explanation: 'Turned down' is a phrasal verb meaning to reject an offer, request, or application, such as a manuscript.

question:
Rearrange the given parts in the correct sequence to form a meaningful sentence.

(A) long-term psychological impact
(B) affecting teenagers, leading to severe emotional
(C) cyberbullying has become a grave issue
(D) distress, social isolation, and in some cases
explanation: The logical sequence is: 'Cyberbullying has become a grave issue (C) affecting teenagers, leading to severe emotional (B) distress, social isolation, and in some cases (D) long-term psychological impact (A).'

question:
Identify the option closest in meaning to the underlined word:

Her presence was acknowledged with a ‘perfunctory’ smile.
explanation: 'Perfunctory' means an action or gesture carried out with a minimum of effort or reflection, showing no real interest. 'Indifferent' is the closest synonym.

question:
Complete the sentences given in List-I with the appropriate adverbs given in List-II

List-I (Sentences)
(A) I ________ drive after sunset.
(B) She has been ________ absenting herself from the office.
(C) The train will arrive ________.
(D) I am ________ satisfied with her conduct.

List-II (Adverbs)
(I) shortly
(II) seldom
(III) much
(IV) frequently
explanation: I 'seldom' (rarely) drive (A-II). 'Frequently' absenting (B-IV). Arrive 'shortly' (C-I). 'Much' satisfied (D-III).

question:
Select the correct antonym of the underlined word from the options given below:

There was a common belief that the resignation of the financial director would put the company in a very ‘vulnerable’ position.
explanation: 'Vulnerable' means susceptible to physical or emotional attack or harm. The antonym is 'invincible', meaning too powerful to be defeated or overcome.

question:
Rearrange the given parts in the correct sequence to form a meaningful sentence.

(A) the balance of nature and ensuring that
(B) conserving wildlife is essential to maintaining
(C) the beauty of diverse animal species
(D) future generations can experience
explanation: The correct grammatical flow is: 'Conserving wildlife is essential to maintaining (B) the balance of nature and ensuring that (A) future generations can experience (D) the beauty of diverse animal species (C).'

question:
Rearrange the following parts in the correct sequence to form a meaningful sentence:

(A) library, dust motes swirled in beams of golden
(B) within the fragile, timeworn pages
(C) in the hushed stillness of the abandoned
(D) light, guarding the untold stories locked
explanation: The sentence reads smoothly as: 'In the hushed stillness of the abandoned (C) library, dust motes swirled in beams of golden (A) light, guarding the untold stories locked (D) within the fragile, timeworn pages (B).'

question:
Select the most appropriate word to fill in the blank in the following sentence.

There ___ to be serious flaws in the design.
explanation: The passive construction 'there are/were said to be' is grammatically correct to report a general belief or claim about something existing.

question:
Fill in the blanks in List-I with the correct homonyms from List-II.

List-I (Sentences)
(A) Honesty is a core _________.
(B) The ________ of the school addressed the students during the assembly.
(C) The car remained ________ in the parking lot.
(D) I bought new ________ like pens and notebooks for the new term.

List-II (Words)
(I) stationary
(II) stationery
(III) principal
(IV) principle
explanation: A 'principle' is a fundamental truth or belief (A-IV). 'Principal' is the head of a school (B-III). 'Stationary' means not moving (C-I). 'Stationery' refers to writing materials like pens (D-II).

question:
Fill in the blank with the appropriate option:

The idiom ‘To Take a rain check’ means ______.
options: to keep something until one needs it
postpone an invitation until a time in the future
to feel in good form or in good health
come to a state of crisis when urgent action is needed
explanation: The idiom 'take a rain check' means to politely decline an offer or invitation now, with the implication that you will accept it at a later date.

question:
Match the blanks in List-I with the appropriate phrases in List-II

List-I (Sentences)
(A) As a member of the army, he felt proud fighting ____ his country.
(B) The company suffered great losses ____ his negligence.
(C) The queen abdicated her throne ____ her son.
(D) I can't just ____ whatever you are planning.

List-II (Phrases)
(I) on behalf of
(II) in favour of
(III) on account of
(IV) go along with
explanation: Fighting 'on behalf of' (representing) his country (A-I). Losses 'on account of' (because of) negligence (B-III). Abdicating 'in favour of' (giving to) her son (C-II). Can't 'go along with' (agree to) the planning (D-IV).

question: Choose the word closest in meaning to the word - salubrious from the options given below.
explanation: 'Salubrious' means health-giving or pleasant and agreeable. Therefore, 'pleasant' is the closest synonym.

question:
Complete the sentences given in List-I with the correct pair given in List-II

List-I (Sentences)
(A) I know I'm ____ in my 40s, but I _____ imagined that I would need glasses.
(B) I _____ kept trying to read the newspaper by holding it ___ away.
(C) I really had to decide whether to get ___ arms or a pair of ___ glasses.
(D) _______, I chose the _______ solution.

List-II (Options)
(I) just / further
(II) eventually / easier
(III) already / never
(IV) longer / reading
explanation: Context dictates: 'already in my 40s/never imagined' (A-III); 'just kept trying/further away' (B-I); 'longer arms/reading glasses' (C-IV); 'eventually/easier solution' (D-II).

question:
Rearrange the given parts in the correct sequence to form a meaningful sentence.

(A) after winning the prize they often publish less frequently
(B) studies of Nobel laureates show that
(C) or encounter professional envy and rivalry
(D) and have strained relations with friends and colleagues
explanation: The correct logical flow is: 'Studies of Nobel laureates show that (B) after winning the prize they often publish less frequently (A) and have strained relations with friends and colleagues (D) or encounter professional envy and rivalry (C).'

question:
Choose the most appropriate word to fill in the blank :

He ____ madness in order to escape.
explanation: To 'feign' means to pretend to be affected by something (a feeling, state, or injury). He pretended or faked madness to escape.

question:
Rearrange the following parts in the correct sequence to form a meaningful sentence:

(A) energy from the sun and break down carbon dioxide and water vapor
(B) easily Nature's biggest achievement comes from her solar energy department,
(C) to make food,while giving out oxygen as a waste product
(D) where she has developed plant cells called chloroplast which take light
explanation: The logical sequence is: 'Easily Nature's biggest achievement comes from her solar energy department, (B) where she has developed plant cells called chloroplast which take light (D) energy from the sun and break down carbon dioxide and water vapor (A) to make food, while giving out oxygen as a waste product (C).'

question:
Choose the most appropriate antonym for the underlined word:

He is an ‘astute’ observer of human behaviour.
explanation: 'Astute' means having or showing an ability to accurately assess situations; sharp or shrewd. Its antonym is 'ingenuous', which means innocent, naive, and unsuspecting.

question:
Fill in the blanks in List-I with the appropriate preposition in List-II

List-I (Sentences)
(A) Akhila sat quietly ___ her best friend during the entire lecture.
(B) ___ being an excellent student, Atul is also a talented musician.
(C) The debate continued ______ the members of the council late into the night.
(D) The lone traveler stood ______ the towering mountains and the river lost in thought.

List-II (Prepositions)
(I) besides
(II) amongst
(III) between
(IV) beside
explanation: Sat 'beside' (next to) her friend (A-IV). 'Besides' (in addition to) being a student (B-I). 'Amongst' (surrounded by) the members (C-II). Stood 'between' two distinct things (mountains and river) (D-III).

question:
Select the most appropriate word to complete the following sentence.

It's quite a ___ subject, so take care how you bring it up.
explanation: A 'delicate' subject is one that is sensitive and needs to be handled carefully and tactfully to avoid causing offense or distress.

question:
Select the most appropriate preposition from the given options to fill in the blank.

The fact that Kim was offered a job has no connection ____ the fact that her cousin is the Managing Director.
explanation: The standard preposition used to link two related (or unrelated) ideas following the noun 'connection' is 'with' (e.g., 'no connection with').

question:
Match the blanks in List-I with the appropriate phrasal verbs from List-II

List-I (Sentences)
(A) The draught was so strong that it _______ the candle.
(B) An argument _______ at the end of the meeting, and it ended on a sour note.
(C) He was very ill last year, but thank goodness, he has _______.
(D) The bus _______ sharply at a red light and I lost my balance and fell over.

List-II (Phrasal Verbs)
(I) pulled through
(II) pulled up
(III) blew out
(IV) blew up
explanation: Wind 'blew out' the candle (A-III). An argument 'blew up' (erupted) (B-IV). To recover from severe illness is to 'pull through' (C-I). A vehicle stopping sharply is 'pulled up' (D-II).

question: Choose the word similar in meaning to the word -Lugubrious
explanation: 'Lugubrious' means looking or sounding sad and dismal. 'Melancholy' is the closest synonym.

question:
Select the most appropriate word to fill in the blank.

Someone who worries too much about small details or rules is said to be ____.
explanation: 'Pedantic' describes someone who is excessively concerned with minor details, rules, or displaying academic learning.

question:
Replace the underlined word in the following sentence with an antonym from the options given:

The ‘mellifluous’ strains of the song floated into the hall, stirring the complacent emotions of many.
explanation: 'Mellifluous' means sweet or musical; pleasant to hear. Its direct antonym is 'harsh', meaning grating or unpleasantly rough.

question: Select the word closest in meaning to the word- Impertinent.
explanation: 'Impertinent' means not showing proper respect; rude. 'Impolite' is the closest synonym.

question:
Match the proverbs in List-I with the meanings in List-II

List-I (Proverbs)
(A) A stitch in time saves nine
(B) Actions speak louder than words
(C) Rome wasn't built in a day
(D) Don't count your chickens before they hatch

List-II (Meanings)
(I) Good deeds are more important than promises
(II) Great achievements take time
(III) Don't plan for something that isn't certain
(IV) Timely action prevents bigger problems
explanation: 'A stitch in time' means timely action (A-IV). 'Actions speak louder' means deeds matter more than promises (B-I). 'Rome wasn't built' means achievements take time (C-II). 'Don't count your chickens' means don't depend on uncertain plans (D-III).

question:
Select the appropriate option to fill in the blank in the following sentence:

Everything is going well. We____ any problems so far.
explanation: The phrase 'so far' requires the present perfect tense to describe an action from the past continuing to the present. 'Haven't had' is the correct form.

question:
Rearrange the following parts in the correct sequence to form a meaningful sentence:

(A) buying choices on the environment they are increasingly
(B) align with their values, demonstrating a commitment to sustainability
(C) as consumers become aware of the influence of their
(D) interested in being associated with brands that
explanation: The logical sequence is: 'As consumers become aware of the influence of their (C) buying choices on the environment they are increasingly (A) interested in being associated with brands that (D) align with their values, demonstrating a commitment to sustainability (B).'
